sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution approving three side letters with Berkeley Fire Fighters Association
to (1) amend Section 33 of the union’s Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with the
City to modify the Firefighter probationary period to 18 months; (2) amend Section
34.3.2 of the MOU to allow employees on 40-hour-week schedules to attend pre-
promotional academies if placed on Outside the City status; and (3) amend Sections
19.4 and 19.5 of the MOU to create a new structure for vacation slots.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
There are no anticipated fiscal impacts, as the recommendations pertain to operational
changes.
sections.current_situation:
The probationary period for newly-hired Firefighters is currently 24 months. The first
side letter (Exhibit A) would modify the probationary period to 18 months, and better
align the Berkeley Fire Department with nearby jurisdictions, which similarly have 18-
month probationary periods (e.g., Oakland, Richmond) and even 12-month probationary
periods (e.g., San Francisco, Hayward, San Rafael).
The second side letter (Exhibit B) seeks to create parity between BFFA members who
are on 56-hour schedules and those who work 40-hour schedules by specifying that 40-
hour-a-week employees can attend pre-promotional academies while on duty if their
supervisor approves and places them on Outside the City (OSC) status.
The third side letter seeks to simplify the current process for Firefighter vacation slots,
which can be challenging to track. The modified process allows for a total of five
vacation slots per vacation period and specifies that no more than four Firefighters or
three Captains can take time off simultaneously.

Berkeley Fire Fighters Association – Side Letters CONSENT CALENDAR
March 11, 2025
sections.background:
The proposed side letters were considered by Council in closed session on February 3,
2025.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects or opportunities associated with the
subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
Without the change in the probationary period, Berkeley Fire Department would
continue to be at a recruitment disadvantage, as candidates would gravitate to agencies
that offer quicker paths to career/permanent status.
The side letter pertaining to pre-promotional academies would create parity between
employees on 40-hour schedules and 56-hour schedules, while the side letter pertaining
to vacation slots would simplify a complicated process that has proven difficult to track.
sections.alternatives:
None.
CONTACT PERSON
Monica Walker, Interim Director of Human Resources, (510) 981-6807.

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
BERKELEY FIRE FIGHTERS ASSOCIATION – SIDE LETTERS PERTAINING TO
PROBATIONARY PERIODS, PRE-PROMOTIONAL ACADEMIES, AND VACATION
SLOTS
WHEREAS, the current probationary period for Firefighters newly-hired into the
Berkeley Fire Department is 24 months, which is longer than that of nearby jurisdictions,
which have 18-month probationary periods (e.g., Oakland, Richmond) and even 12-
month probationary periods (e.g., San Francisco, Hayward, San Rafael); and
WHEREAS, modifying the probationary period for Firefighters would align the City of
Berkeley with nearby jurisdictions and eliminate a recruitment disadvantage that the
Berkeley Fire Department suffers when competing for talent against jurisdictions that
offer candidates quicker paths to career/permanent status; and
WHEREAS, the Berkeley Fire Department seeks to create parity between Berkeley Fire
Fighters Association (BFFA) members who are on 56-hour schedules and those who
work 40-hour weeks by specifying that 40-hour-a-week employees can attend pre-
promotional academies while on duty if their supervisor approves and places them on
Outside the City (OSC) status; and
WHEREAS, the current process for Firefighter vacation slots can be challenging to
track, necessitating a modified process; and
WHEREAS, the City and BFFA have agreed upon a new process to track vacation
slots, allowing for a total of five vacation slots per vacation period and specifying that no
more than four Firefighters or three Captains can take time off simultaneously;
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is hereby authorized to execute a side letter with Berkeley Fire Fighters
Association, which is attached hereto, made a part hereof and marked Exhibit A, to
amend
